fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to georgia, speech-language pathologist compact georgia, georgia speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Professional Licensing Boards Division, Office of the Secretary of State

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Georgia. Requires a master's degree, completed professional experience, passing Praxis score, and background check.

license_types.title: PCE/RPE License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Paid Clinical Experience (PCE) or Required Professional Experience (RPE). Application fee: $40. Allows supervised practice while completing the clinical fellowship.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the Georgia Online Licensing System and complete the online application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have your official graduate transcripts, verifying a Master's or Doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology, sent directly from your educational institution to the Georgia Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Verify Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Provide verification of your post-graduate professional experience (e.g., ASHA Clinical Fellowship Year) as required by the Board. This may involve submitting specific forms from your supervisor or ASHA directly to the Board.

steps.title: Pass Praxis Examination
steps.description: Ensure your official scores for the Praxis Series Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (Test #5331) are sent directly to the Board from ETS.

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Register for a fingerprint-based background check through Gemalto Cogent, following the specific instructions and service code provided by the Board for applicants.

documents.detail: From an accredited speech-language pathology program.
Score of 162 or higher, as approved by the Board.
Documentation of completed PCE/RPE signed by supervising licensed SLP.
Fingerprinting through Georgia Crime Information Center (GCIC) and FBI. Applicant is responsible for associated fees.
If previously licensed in another state, verification sent directly from the issuing board to Georgia.

telepractice_text: Georgia permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Georgia SLP license to provide services to clients located in Georgia. All telepractice services must comply with Georgia telehealth regulations and meet the same standard of care as in-person services.
provisional_text: Georgia issues a PCE/RPE License for graduates completing their clinical fellowship. The license costs $40 and allows supervised practice. The Board reviews applications monthly, so processing time depends on the Board's meeting schedule.
reciprocity_text: Georgia offers licensure by endorsement for SLPs licensed in other states, as well as for ASHA CCC-SLP holders. The endorsement application fee is $120 (same as by examination). Out-of-state applicants must have their existing license verified directly by the issuing state board.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $120 (includes $10 mail-in processing). A PCE/RPE license for the clinical fellowship costs $40. Biennial renewal is $70, due by March 31 of odd-numbered years.

faqs.question: What are the CE requirements for Georgia SLP renewal?
faqs.answer: Georgia requires 2.0 CEUs (equivalent to 20 clock hours) every two years. A maximum of 2 hours per cycle can be in ethics/documentation topics. No more than 10 clock hours can be acquired in any one 24-hour period. Requirements are pro-rated for new licensees.

faqs.question: How long does the Georgia SLP application take?
faqs.answer: Application processing takes 4 to 8 weeks. The Board meets monthly to review applications, so timing depends on when your complete application is received relative to the next Board meeting.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Georgia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Georgia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Georgia offers licensure by endorsement for SLPs licensed in other states, as well as for ASHA CCC-SLP holders. The endorsement application fee is $120 (same as by examination). Out-of-state applicants must have their existing license verified directly by the issuing state board.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Georgia
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Georgia, submit your application and all required supporting documentation through the Georgia Online Licensing System.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Georgia Online Licensing System, click 'Register a Person', then 'Click Here to Register'. You will need to provide your Social Security Number, last name, and date of birth to create your account and login credentials.
